acceptance_targets: [chapter_13-F001] PASS if revised lines around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:359-363 include at least one explicit quoted expletive from Tom directed at the conflict, plus a concrete reaction beat from Elsie; FAIL if the moment is still rendered as summary phrasing like 'swore under his breath.'

[chapter_13-F002] PASS if, before the chapter ends, a non-household actor creates a new problem on-page in the revised passages around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:385-547 and Elsie takes a specific action that worsens one other variable (money, reputation, or medical access); FAIL if those passages remain planning-only with no new external pressure.

[chapter_13-F003] PASS if, in revised dialogue around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:452-494, at least three Noreen lines use a distinct rhythmic pivot/interruption or humor beat that Elsie does not use, and no Noreen line mirrors Elsie's numbered-instruction syntax; FAIL if both voices still read as interchangeable procedural speech.

[chapter_13-F004] PASS if revised passages around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:389-399 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:536-547 retain the same information but are rendered as continuous action/thought beats with at least one external interruption in each passage; FAIL if both remain formatted or cadenced as sequential list entries.

[chapter_13-F005] PASS if only one refusal remains a bare 'No' in the revised spans and the other refusal beats add new tactical information or pressure through wording/action; FAIL if three or more refusals still appear as near-identical standalone negatives.

[chapter_13-F006] PASS if the final two paragraphs in the revised section around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:553-565 end on a specific unresolved risk cue (sound, symptom, knock, or material shortfall) with no summarizing reassurance that resolve 'holds'; FAIL if the ending still closes on abstract emotional stabilization.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:359-363 include at least one explicit quoted expletive from Tom directed at the conflict, plus a concrete reaction beat from Elsie; FAIL if the moment is still rendered as summary phrasing like 'swore under his breath.'
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at peak pressure: the narration uses the euphemistic summary 'He swore under his breath' instead of rendering the profanity and social force directly. In this confrontation, that softens conflict impact and undercuts Tom's high-profanity stress register.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the beat around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:359 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:361 so Tom's profanity is spoken on-page in quoted dialogue, with immediate embodied fallout (what Elsie hears/sees and what the thin-walled house would plausibly carry). Do not summarize the profanity as narration.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, before the chapter ends, a non-household actor creates a new problem on-page in the revised passages around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:385-547 and Elsie takes a specific action that worsens one other variable (money, reputation, or medical access); FAIL if those passages remain planning-only with no new external pressure.
findings.problem: After Tom exits, the chapter shifts into extended planning and script rehearsal without a fresh external complication. Consequence is described but not re-pressurized on-page, so scene energy drops before the close.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the post-departure stretch at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:385 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:536, add a concrete outside pressure event (e.g., depot contact, creditor runner, landlord probe, or witness confrontation) that forces Elsie into an immediate choice with cost. Keep the logistics, but make at least one plan fail in real time.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in revised dialogue around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:452-494, at least three Noreen lines use a distinct rhythmic pivot/interruption or humor beat that Elsie does not use, and no Noreen line mirrors Elsie's numbered-instruction syntax; FAIL if both voices still read as interchangeable procedural speech.
findings.problem: Elsie and Noreen intermittently collapse into the same procedural/legal cadence (numbered directives, evidentiary phrasing, risk scripting), which blurs idiolect separation in a high-stakes exchange.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework Noreen's lines in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:452 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:490 so her rhythm carries her profile traits (quick pivots, humor under strain, socially deft phrasing) while Elsie keeps blunt contractual boundaries in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:261. Preserve content, separate syntax and cadence.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised passages around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:389-399 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:536-547 retain the same information but are rendered as continuous action/thought beats with at least one external interruption in each passage; FAIL if both remain formatted or cadenced as sequential list entries.
findings.problem: A recurring ledger/list construction appears more than twice (inline account lists, numbered instruction lists, scripted bullet-like statements). The repetition reads as over-literal reuse of stress-tell tokens and flattens chapter rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one deliberate list motif at the opening (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:9-11), but convert later list-heavy blocks at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:389-399 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:536-547 into scene-active prose with interruption, sensory pressure, and micro-conflict rather than stacked entries.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if only one refusal remains a bare 'No' in the revised spans and the other refusal beats add new tactical information or pressure through wording/action; FAIL if three or more refusals still appear as near-identical standalone negatives.
findings.problem: Recurring refusal formula ('No' as a standalone line/beat) appears more than twice and starts to feel mechanical rather than escalating. The repeated shape reduces tonal variation in the confrontation.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the refusal beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:155, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:159, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:365, vary at least two refusals with differentiated diction, interruption, or embodied counterpressure so each beat changes leverage rather than repeating a fixed cadence.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the final two paragraphs in the revised section around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:553-565 end on a specific unresolved risk cue (sound, symptom, knock, or material shortfall) with no summarizing reassurance that resolve 'holds'; FAIL if the ending still closes on abstract emotional stabilization.
findings.problem: Timidity at the close: the ending resolves into declarative steadiness ('the words... held') after mounting legal, financial, and medical threat, which functions as a tension safety valve instead of leaving embodied uncertainty active.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the closing movement around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:553-565 so Elsie's resolve is tested by a concrete physical or social threat beat in the room/street, and end on unresolved consequence rather than a stabilizing assurance sentence.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the more specific and potentially uncomfortable rendering if it is character-true and causally necessary. Intimacy, bodily risk, and moral compromise must be shown with concrete detail and aftermath, then integrated into later behavior rather than quarantined as a one-scene shock.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft abstracts pain, sex, coercion, blood, or economic shame at the exact moment consequence should be embodied, revise by replacing summary with physically and socially specific action tied to POV stakes.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High by default for spoken lines; uncontracted forms are marked choices for period formality, emphasis, or character-specific control behavior.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural engines across adjacent chapters: ensemble social scenes, inventory chapters, information-turn chapters, acted-upon pressure chapters, and aftermath chapters. Vary scene count (2 to 4), dialogue density, and interiority depth so consecutive chapters do not share the same pacing signature.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-authentic but readable contemporary English, favoring concrete nouns and precise verbs over lyrical abstraction.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating primarily between Elsie and Noreen, with occasional tightly limited deviations only when causal clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Sentence rhythm should alternate compressed pressure bursts with longer observational lines that carry social texture; avoid monotone paragraph cadence.
